gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/405)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/405)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) أَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) يَنْهَى عَنْ قِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ فِي الْحَمَّامِ قَالَ لَا إِنَّمَا نَهَى أَنْ يَقْرَأَ الرَّجُلُ وَ هُوَ عُرْيَانٌ فَأَمَّا إِذَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِ إِزَارٌ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, form his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Rabi’e Bin Abdullah, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Was Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> forbidding from reciting the Quran in the bathhouse?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but rather he<sup>asws</sup> had forbidden that the man should be reciting whilst he is naked. So when there was a trouser upon him, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 31. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from ’Isma‘il ibn Mehran from Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah from Ali ibn Yaqtin who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘Can I read Quran in a bathhouse or do sex?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful.’”
